message.content: Investigate READ-ONLY why the glm-5.2 card audit hung. It stopped at 175/5759 and its log (/tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/audit_run.log) is now full of "Task was destroyed but it is pending!". The audit tooling is in scripts/card_audit/ (harness.py, tier0.py, disposition.py, judge_guard.py). It was repointed from claude-p-opus to OpenRouter glm-5.2. Determine: (1) the ROOT CAUSE of the asyncio hang — is it asyncio.run()/async OpenRouterClient called inside ThreadPoolExecutor worker threads (event loop destroyed → pending tasks killed)? Read harness.py's transport and the orchestrator the agent built. (2) Did the audit produce ANYTHING usable before hanging (the earlier report showed 100% quarantine — a separate calibration bug)? (3) Is it checkpoint-resumable from 175? (4) What is the CORRECT transport for glm-5.2 in a threaded worker (a synchronous requests/httpx POST per call, not the async client)? Recommend the fix shape (not implementation). Do NOT modify anything or kill any process.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Tier-1/2/3 Opus semantic audit harness (Sol §3 "Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3", §Faithfulness, §Numeric
3	fidelity, §Relevance, §Voice, §4 disposition rules).
4	
5	Where Tier-0 (`card_audit.tier0`) decides everything provable OFFLINE on structure alone, THIS module
6	runs the parts that need a model — but it never lets the model manufacture an admit a deterministic gate
7	already refused:
8	
9	  FAITHFULNESS is NOT reinvented. The authoritative faithfulness verdict is `report_ast.entailed_by_span`
10	  called on the exact verified span bytes and the canonically-normalized claim (Sol §Faithfulness step 4,
11	  and §1 "reuse the entailment judge"). A report-AST FAIL is decisive and CANNOT be overridden by an Opus
12	  opinion (Sol §Faithfulness, §Tier 3). Opus is asked for its atom-level opinion too, but a card whose
13	  span does not entail its claim fails no matter what Opus says.
14	
15	  TIER 1 / TIER 2 are two INDEPENDENT Opus passes (Sol §Tier 1, §Tier 2). Each is a fresh, self-contained
16	  `claude -p --model opus --effort max` session with a read-only packet: no Agent/Bash/Edit/Write/web
17	  tools, no access to the other pass's verdict, and a JSON-schema-constrained structured receipt — never
18	  a reasoning trace (Sol §"Efficient execution", §Phase 2). There is NO cheaper fallback: a provider
19	  failure retries the SAME model up to N times and then fails closed; Sonnet/Haiku/GLM/"deterministic
20	  pass" are never substituted (Sol §"Efficient execution").
21	
22	  TIER 3 adjudicates (Sol §Tier 3) when the two passes disagree, either fails, report-AST passes but Opus
23	  alleges an unsupported atom, or a repair/edge-removal/OWNED-demotion is proposed. The adjudicator may
24	  overturn an Opus FALSE POSITIVE, but it CANNOT override a failed byte binding or a failed
25	  `entailed_by_span` — those require a repaired card and a full rerun.
26	
27	GENERALITY (Sol Phase 8): there is not one DOI, title, subject, venue or benchmark literal in this file
28	or in the prompts it builds. Every injected value (research question, contract facets, span, card fields)
29	is a parameter, so a clinical / legal / economics / CS corpus is audited by the identical harness. The
30	legacy `scripts/quarantine.py` is deliberately NOT imported (Sol §1: it hardcodes journal-only policy).
31	
32	The Opus transport is INJECTABLE (`OpusRunner`). Production wires `subprocess_opus_runner`, which shells
33	`claude -p`; tests inject a deterministic stub, exactly as `report_ast.set_entailment_judge` lets the
34	faithfulness judge be stubbed. So the whole tier ladder is validated hermetically without a real call.
35	"""
36	from __future__ import annotations
37	
38	import json
39	import re
40	import shutil
41	import subprocess
42	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
43	from pathlib import Path
44	
45	import provenance as P
46	import report_ast as RA
47	
48	from card_audit.audit_schema import PASS, FAIL, UNCERTAIN, NOT_APPLICABLE
49	from card_audit.tier0 import canonicalize
50	
51	# =================================================================================================
52	# Constants: passes, voice, the closed relevance / disposition / numeric-subdimension vocabularies
53	# =================================================================================================
54	PASS_A = 'A'          # Tier-1 first Opus pass
55	PASS_B = 'B'          # Tier-2 second, independent Opus pass
56	PASS_ADJ = 'ADJ'      # Tier-3 adjudication
57	
58	# Sol §Voice: the current v2 schema has no voice field, so every incoming card is normalized ATTRIBUTED.
59	# A failing card is NEVER silently reinterpreted as OWNED; only an explicit repair disposition may create
60	# an OWNED suggestion, and that is not citeable evidence.
61	VOICE_ATTRIBUTED = 'ATTRIBUTED'
62	VOICE_OWNED = 'OWNED'
63	
64	# Sol §Faithfulness: an ATTRIBUTED card that is structurally unreachable (span cannot be resolved) still
65	# gets the other semantic judgments, but its faithfulness verdict is UNREACHABLE — never PASS.
66	FAITH_UNREACHABLE = 'UNREACHABLE'
67	
68	# Sol §Relevance: pass ONLY these two classifications; everything else fails.
69	REL_DIRECT = 'DIRECT_ANSWER_EVIDENCE'
70	REL_CONTEXT = 'NECESSARY_CONTEXT'
71	REL_PASS_CLASSES = frozenset({REL_DIRECT, REL_CONTEXT})
72	REL_CLASSES = frozenset({
73	    REL_DIRECT, REL_CONTEXT,
74	    'ADJACENT_SUBJECT', 'GENERIC_FILLER', 'OTHER_QUESTION', 'METADATA_OR_METHODS_ONLY',
75	    'RELEVANCE_OUTSIDE_BINDING',
76	})
77	
78	# Sol §Numeric fidelity: all twelve subdimensions must pass for a numeric claim.
79	NUMERIC_SUBDIMENSIONS = (
80	    'number', 'direction', 'magnitude', 'unit', 'comparator', 'population',
81	    'geography', 'period', 'scope', 'uncertainty', 'modality', 'precision',
82	)
83	
84	# Sol §4: the closed disposition vocabulary. There is no DELETE.
85	DISPOSITIONS = frozenset({
86	    'KEEP_UNCHANGED', 'REPAIR_TIGHTEN', 'REBASE_TO_VALID_SUPPORT', 'REMOVE_BAD_SUPPORT_EDGE',
87	    'DEMOTE_TO_OWNED_SUGGESTION', 'QUARANTINE_CARD', 'QUARANTINE_SUPPORT_EDGE',
88	})
89	
90	# Stable machine reason codes this harness cites (Sol §Report: quarantine counts by stable reason code).
91	RC_FAITH_NOT_ENTAILED = 'faithfulness.span_does_not_entail_claim'
92	RC_FAITH_UNREACHABLE = 'faithfulness.span_unreachable'
93	RC_FAITH_NO_CLAIM = 'faithfulness.no_claim_to_test'
94	RC_CORR_NOT_ENTAILED = 'faithfulness.corroborator_does_not_entail_primary'
95	RC_OPUS_SCHEMA = 'opus.response_failed_schema'
96	RC_OPUS_MODEL = 'opus.model_not_verified_as_opus'
97	RC_OPUS_TRANSPORT = 'opus.transport_failed_after_retries'
98	RC_OPUS_DISAGREE = 'opus.pass_a_b_disagree'
99	RC_OPUS_ALLEGES_ATOM = 'opus.alleges_unsupported_atom_reportast_passed'
100	RC_OPUS_RELEVANCE = 'opus.card_not_relevant'
101	RC_OPUS_NUMERIC = 'opus.numeric_atom_failed'
102	RC_OPUS_COT = 'opus.field_not_clean_content_class'
103	RC_OPUS_FACET = 'opus.facet_tag_unsupported'
104	RC_VOICE_LAUNDER = 'voice.attributed_to_owned_launder_blocked'
105	
106	_LEGAL_VERDICTS = frozenset({PASS, FAIL, UNCERTAIN, NOT_APPLICABLE})
107	
108	
109	# =================================================================================================
110	# Faithfulness — REUSE report_ast.entailed_by_span, never reinvent (Sol §1, §Faithfulness)
111	# =================================================================================================
112	def resolve_verified_span(card: dict, graph: P.Graph) -> str | None:
113	    """Sol §Faithfulness steps 1-2: resolve the EXACT raw binding and return
114	    `manifestation.text[span_start:span_end]` — but only if the binding actually verifies against its
115	    bytes. A span that does not verify is structurally unreachable, not admissible evidence, and its
116	    faithfulness verdict is UNREACHABLE (never tested against `card['span']`, wider context, or another
117	    source). Returns None when unreachable."""
118	    binding = RA._binding_from_card(card)
119	    if not binding:
120	        return None
121	    try:
122	        if not graph.verify_span(binding):
123	            return None
124	    except Exception:                                   # noqa: BLE001 — an unverifiable binding is unreachable
125	        return None
126	    m = graph.manifestations.get(binding['manifestation_id'])
127	    if m is None:
128	        return None
129	    s, e = card.get('span_start'), card.get('span_end')
130	    if not isinstance(s, int) or not isinstance(e, int) or isinstance(s, bool) or isinstance(e, bool):
131	        return None
132	    text = m.text or ''
133	    if s < 0 or e > len(text) or e <= s:
134	        return None
135	    return text[s:e]
136	
137	
138	def _work_for(card: dict, graph: P.Graph) -> P.Work | None:
139	    binding = RA._binding_from_card(card)
140	    if binding:
141	        m = graph.manifestations.get(binding.get('manifestation_id'))
142	        if m is not None:
143	            return graph.works.get(m.work_id)
144	    return graph.works.get(card.get('work_id') or '')
145	
146	
147	@dataclass
148	class FaithfulnessReceipt:
149	    """The report-AST faithfulness result for one claim/span pair (primary or one corroborator). This is
150	    AUTHORITATIVE: a FAIL here cannot be overridden by Opus (Sol §Faithfulness, §Tier 3)."""
151	    verdict: str                       # PASS / FAIL / NOT_APPLICABLE
152	    faith_label: str                   # ENTAILED / NOT_ENTAILED / UNREACHABLE / NO_CLAIM
153	    reason_codes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
154	    detail: str = ''
155	    role: str = 'primary'              # 'primary' | 'corroborator'
156	
157	    def to_json(self) -> dict:
158	        return dict(verdict=self.verdict, faith_label=self.faith_label,
159	                    reason_codes=list(self.reason_codes), detail=self.detail, role=self.role)
160	
161	
162	def audit_faithfulness_primary(card: dict, graph: P.Graph) -> FaithfulnessReceipt:
163	    """Sol §Faithfulness: the primary claim must be ENTAILED by its OWN bound span. Normalize the claim
164	    exactly as the composer does (HTML-unescape, collapse whitespace, strip a single trailing period) and
165	    call `report_ast.entailed_by_span(normalized_claim, resolved_span, work)`. Pass ONLY on (True, '')."""
166	    claim = canonicalize(card.get('claim') or '')
167	    if not claim:
168	        return FaithfulnessReceipt(NOT_APPLICABLE, 'NO_CLAIM', [RC_FAITH_NO_CLAIM],
169	                                   'card carries no claim to test', 'primary')
170	    span = resolve_verified_span(card, graph)
171	    if span is None:
172	        return FaithfulnessReceipt(FAIL, FAITH_UNREACHABLE, [RC_FAITH_UNREACHABLE],
173	                                   'span structurally unreachable — binding does not verify', 'primary')
174	    ok, why = RA.entailed_by_span(claim, span, _work_for(card, graph))
175	    if ok:
176	        return FaithfulnessReceipt(PASS, 'ENTAILED', [], '', 'primary')
177	    return FaithfulnessReceipt(FAIL, 'NOT_ENTAILED', [RC_FAITH_NOT_ENTAILED],
178	                               f'entailed_by_span rejected: {why}', 'primary')
179	
180	
181	def audit_faithfulness_corroborators(card: dict, graph: P.Graph) -> list[FaithfulnessReceipt]:
182	    """Sol §Faithfulness: "A corroborating span must INDEPENDENTLY entail the primary claim. It never
183	    inherits the primary card's pass." So each corroborator's own verified span is tested against the
184	    PRIMARY card's normalized claim — never against the corroborator's own restatement, never concatenated
185	    with the primary span (that recreates evidence laundering)."""
186	    claim = canonicalize(card.get('claim') or '')
187	    out: list[FaithfulnessReceipt] = []
188	    for i, edge in enumerate(card.get('corroborating_sources') or []):
189	        if not claim:
190	            out.append(FaithfulnessReceipt(NOT_APPLICABLE, 'NO_CLAIM', [RC_FAITH_NO_CLAIM],
191	                                           'primary carries no claim to corroborate', 'corroborator'))
192	            continue
193	        span = resolve_verified_span(edge, graph)
194	        if span is None:
195	            out.append(FaithfulnessReceipt(FAIL, FAITH_UNREACHABLE, [RC_FAITH_UNREACHABLE],
196	                                           f'corroborator[{i}] span unreachable', 'corroborator'))
197	            continue
198	        ok, why = RA.entailed_by_span(claim, span, _work_for(edge, graph))
199	        if ok:
200	            out.append(FaithfulnessReceipt(PASS, 'ENTAILED', [], f'corroborator[{i}]', 'corroborator'))
201	        else:
202	            out.append(FaithfulnessReceipt(FAIL, 'NOT_ENTAILED', [RC_CORR_NOT_ENTAILED],
203	                                           f'corroborator[{i}] does not entail primary: {why}',
204	                                           'corroborator'))
205	    return out

217	def voice_launder_blocked(final_disposition: str, faith: FaithfulnessReceipt) -> bool:
218	    """Sol §Voice / §4 DEMOTE_TO_OWNED_SUGGESTION: a FAILING card must never be laundered into an OWNED
219	    suggestion that keeps its particulars. A demotion is only sound when the faithfulness failure is not

226	# =================================================================================================
227	# The Opus structured-verdict schema and its validator (Sol §Phase 2: validate before accepting)
228	# =================================================================================================
229	def opus_response_json_schema() -> dict:
230	    """The JSON schema passed to `claude -p --json-schema` AND used to validate every response before it
231	    is accepted (Sol Phase 2 step 6, Phase 3 step 5). Per-dimension objects, machine reason codes, an
232	    unsupported atom + deciding source substring, a content class for every non-empty field, and — for a
233	    numeric claim — every numeric subdimension verdict. No free-form reasoning field exists."""
234	    verdict_enum = sorted(_LEGAL_VERDICTS)
235	    dim = {
236	        'type': 'object', 'additionalProperties': False,
237	        'required': ['verdict'],
238	        'properties': {
239	            'verdict': {'type': 'string', 'enum': verdict_enum},
240	            'reason_code': {'type': 'string'},
241	            'unsupported_atom': {'type': 'string'},
242	            'deciding_substring': {'type': 'string'},
243	            'affected_field': {'type': 'string'},
244	        },
245	    }
246	    numeric = {
247	        'type': 'object', 'additionalProperties': False,
248	        'required': ['applicable', 'subdimensions'],
249	        'properties': {
250	            'applicable': {'type': 'boolean'},
251	            'subdimensions': {
252	                'type': 'object', 'additionalProperties': False,
253	                'properties': {k: {'type': 'string', 'enum': verdict_enum} for k in NUMERIC_SUBDIMENSIONS},
254	            },
255	            'unsupported_atom': {'type': 'string'},
256	            'deciding_substring': {'type': 'string'},
257	        },
258	    }
259	    relevance = {
260	        'type': 'object', 'additionalProperties': False,
261	        'required': ['verdict', 'classification'],
262	        'properties': {
263	            'verdict': {'type': 'string', 'enum': verdict_enum},
264	            'classification': {'type': 'string', 'enum': sorted(REL_CLASSES)},
265	            'reason_code': {'type': 'string'},
266	            'connection_to_question': {'type': 'string'},
267	        },
268	    }
269	    facet = {
270	        'type': 'object', 'additionalProperties': False,
271	        'required': ['verdict'],
272	        'properties': {
273	            'verdict': {'type': 'string', 'enum': verdict_enum},
274	            'unsupported_tags': {'type': 'array', 'items': {'type': 'string'}},
275	        },
276	    }
277	    content_class = {
278	        'type': 'object', 'additionalProperties': False,
279	        'required': ['field', 'content_class', 'verdict'],
280	        'properties': {
281	            'field': {'type': 'string'},
282	            'content_class': {'type': 'string'},
283	            'verdict': {'type': 'string', 'enum': verdict_enum},
284	        },
285	    }
286	    return {
287	        'type': 'object', 'additionalProperties': False,
288	        'required': ['audit_row_id', 'faithfulness', 'numeric', 'relevance', 'facet',
289	                     'content_classes', 'proposed_disposition'],
290	        'properties': {
291	            'audit_row_id': {'type': 'string'},
292	            'faithfulness': dim,
293	            'numeric': numeric,
294	            'relevance': relevance,
295	            'facet': facet,
296	            'content_classes': {'type': 'array', 'items': content_class},
297	            'proposed_disposition': {'type': 'string', 'enum': sorted(DISPOSITIONS)},
298	        },
299	    }

302	class OpusResponseInvalid(Exception):
303	    """A response that fails schema validation. Sol Phase 1/3: missing/uncertain judge results are
304	    FAILURES; a malformed judge output is never read as a pass."""
305	
306	
307	def validate_opus_response(obj, expected_audit_row_id: str) -> dict:
308	    """Validate a parsed Opus response against `opus_response_json_schema` WITHOUT a third-party jsonschema
309	    dependency — a small, explicit, fail-closed checker. Raises OpusResponseInvalid on any deviation, so
310	    an unvalidated response can never enter the verdict set (Sol Phase 3 step 5)."""
311	    if not isinstance(obj, dict):
312	        raise OpusResponseInvalid('response is not a JSON object')
313	    schema = opus_response_json_schema()
314	    for k in schema['required']:
315	        if k not in obj:
316	            raise OpusResponseInvalid(f'missing required key {k!r}')
317	    unknown = set(obj) - set(schema['properties'])
318	    if unknown:
319	        raise OpusResponseInvalid(f'unknown top-level key(s): {sorted(unknown)}')
320	    if obj['audit_row_id'] != expected_audit_row_id:
321	        raise OpusResponseInvalid(
322	            f'audit_row_id {obj["audit_row_id"]!r} != requested {expected_audit_row_id!r}')
323	
324	    def _check_verdict(v, where):
325	        if v not in _LEGAL_VERDICTS:
326	            raise OpusResponseInvalid(f'{where}: illegal verdict {v!r}')
327	
328	    _check_verdict(obj['faithfulness'].get('verdict'), 'faithfulness')
329	
330	    num = obj['numeric']
331	    if not isinstance(num.get('applicable'), bool):
332	        raise OpusResponseInvalid('numeric.applicable must be a bool')
333	    subs = num.get('subdimensions') or {}
334	    if num['applicable']:
335	        for k in NUMERIC_SUBDIMENSIONS:
336	            if k not in subs:
337	                raise OpusResponseInvalid(f'numeric claim missing subdimension {k!r}')
338	            _check_verdict(subs[k], f'numeric.{k}')
339	    for k, v in subs.items():
340	        if k not in NUMERIC_SUBDIMENSIONS:
341	            raise OpusResponseInvalid(f'unknown numeric subdimension {k!r}')
342	        _check_verdict(v, f'numeric.{k}')
343	
344	    rel = obj['relevance']
345	    _check_verdict(rel.get('verdict'), 'relevance')
346	    if rel.get('classification') not in REL_CLASSES:
347	        raise OpusResponseInvalid(f'relevance.classification illegal: {rel.get("classification")!r}')
348	
349	    _check_verdict(obj['facet'].get('verdict'), 'facet')
350	
351	    if not isinstance(obj['content_classes'], list):
352	        raise OpusResponseInvalid('content_classes must be a list')
353	    for cc in obj['content_classes']:
354	        if not isinstance(cc, dict) or {'field', 'content_class', 'verdict'} - set(cc):
355	            raise OpusResponseInvalid('content_classes entry missing field/content_class/verdict')
356	        _check_verdict(cc['verdict'], f'content_class[{cc.get("field")}]')
357	
358	    if obj['proposed_disposition'] not in DISPOSITIONS:
359	        raise OpusResponseInvalid(f'illegal proposed_disposition {obj["proposed_disposition"]!r}')
360	    return obj
361

364	# The Opus transport — INJECTABLE, opus-only, no cheaper fallback (Sol §"Efficient execution")
365	# =================================================================================================
366	class OpusUnavailable(Exception):
367	    """The opus transport could not be used at all (CLI absent, or every retry failed). Fails closed."""
368	
369	
370	def model_is_opus(result: dict) -> bool:
371	    """Sol Phase 3 step 5 / §"Efficient execution": REQUIRE response metadata to prove the requested model
372	    was Opus, and prove it was NOT a cheaper model. Reads the `claude -p --output-format json` envelope:
373	    `modelUsage` is keyed by the model id(s) actually billed, and `model` names the primary model."""
374	    usage = result.get('modelUsage') or {}
375	    hay = ' '.join(list(usage.keys()) + [str(result.get('model') or '')]).lower()
376	    if 'opus' not in hay:
377	        return False                                    # no opus anywhere -> rejects sonnet/haiku/glm-only
378	    # opus IS present. The `claude -p` CLI bills a small auxiliary housekeeping turn on a cheaper model
379	    # (e.g. a haiku title/summary) even under `--model opus`, so mere co-occurrence is not disqualifying.
380	    # Require that the SUBSTANTIVE answer came from opus: the model that produced the most OUTPUT tokens
381	    # must be opus. A decoy where a cheaper model does the real work while opus is billed one token fails.
382	    _CHEAP = ('sonnet', 'haiku', 'glm', 'gpt', 'gemini', 'llama', 'mistral')
383	
384	    def _out(v):
385	        return (v.get('outputTokens') or v.get('output_tokens') or 0) if isinstance(v, dict) else 0
386	
387	    outs = {k.lower(): _out(v) for k, v in usage.items()}
388	    if outs and any(outs.values()):
389	        dominant = max(outs, key=lambda k: outs[k])
390	        return 'opus' in dominant and not any(c in dominant for c in _CHEAP)
391	    # No output-token telemetry (e.g. a test stub): accept only if the primary `model` is not a cheaper one.
392	    primary = str(result.get('model') or '').lower()
393	    return not (primary and any(c in primary for c in _CHEAP))
394

396	# An OpusRunner is a callable(prompt: str, schema: dict) -> dict, returning the parsed `claude -p`
397	# JSON envelope (the object with `result`, `modelUsage`, `is_error`, ...). Production wires
398	# `subprocess_opus_runner`; tests inject a deterministic stub.
399	def subprocess_opus_runner(prompt: str, schema: dict, *, timeout_s: int = 900,
400	                           max_retries: int = 3) -> dict:
401	    """Production transport: a fresh `claude -p --model opus --effort max` session with a read-only,
402	    self-contained packet and NO Agent/Bash/Edit/Write/web/task tools (Sol §"Efficient execution"). A
403	    provider failure retries the SAME model up to `max_retries` times; there is NO degrade to a cheaper
404	    model and NO deterministic "pass" (Sol). Raises OpusUnavailable if it cannot get a valid envelope."""
405	    if not shutil.which('claude'):
406	        raise OpusUnavailable('claude CLI not on PATH')
407	    cmd = [
408	        'claude', '-p',
409	        '--model', 'opus',
410	        '--effort', 'max',
411	        '--permission-mode', 'dontAsk',
412	        '--output-format', 'json',
413	        # This CLI takes the JSON SCHEMA INLINE, not a file path (a path is parsed as JSON and rejected).
414	        '--json-schema', json.dumps(schema),
415	        # a read-only worker: no tools that could edit the repo, touch the mine, or spawn a subagent.
416	        '--disallowedTools', 'Agent,Task,Bash,Edit,Write,WebFetch,WebSearch,AskUserQuestion,ExitPlanMode',
417	    ]
418	    last_err = ''
419	    for _attempt in range(max(1, max_retries)):
420	        try:
421	            r = subprocess.run(cmd, input=prompt.encode('utf-8'), capture_output=True, timeout=timeout_s)
422	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
423	            last_err = f'timeout after {timeout_s}s'
424	            continue
425	        except Exception as e:                          # noqa: BLE001
426	            last_err = f'invocation failed: {e!r}'
427	            continue
428	        try:
429	            envelope = json.loads(r.stdout.decode('utf-8', errors='replace'))
430	        except Exception:                               # noqa: BLE001
431	            last_err = f'non-JSON envelope (rc={r.returncode}): {r.stderr.decode("utf-8", "replace")[:200]}'
432	            continue
433	        if envelope.get('is_error'):
434	            last_err = f'claude reported is_error: {envelope.get("subtype")}'
435	            continue
436	        return envelope
437	    raise OpusUnavailable(f'opus transport failed after {max_retries} retries: {last_err}')
438

468	def build_opus_prompt(packet: dict, schema: dict) -> str:
469	    """The self-contained instruction. It names NO subject, DOI, venue, benchmark, or fixed source policy
470	    — the research question and contract facets are injected (Sol Phase 2 step 5). It demands a JSON
471	    receipt matching `schema` and forbids any prose reasoning trace (Sol §"Efficient execution")."""
472	    return (
473	        'You are a STRICT, independent auditor of one evidence card extracted from a source document for '
474	        'a literature review. You judge ONLY the injected packet below. You may not browse, run tools, or '
475	        'reason aloud. Return ONE JSON object matching the schema and NOTHING else.\n\n'
476	        'Judge these dimensions on the card against its RESOLVED_SPAN (the exact verified source bytes) '
477	        'and the injected RESEARCH_QUESTION and CONTRACT_FACETS:\n'
478	        '  - faithfulness: does the resolved span, read alone, entail the card\'s claim? A dropped '
479	        'qualifier, flipped direction, inflated magnitude, widened scope/population/geography, or '
480	        'upgraded modality is NOT entailed. If the span cannot support an atom, name it in '
481	        'unsupported_atom and the deciding source words in deciding_substring.\n'
482	        '  - numeric: if the claim asserts any quantity, set applicable=true and give a verdict for EVERY '
483	        'subdimension (number, direction, magnitude, unit, comparator, population, geography, period, '
484	        'scope, uncertainty, modality, precision). percent and percentage points are distinct; '
485	        'association is not causation; a forecast is not an observation.\n'
486	        '  - relevance: classify the card. Only DIRECT_ANSWER_EVIDENCE or NECESSARY_CONTEXT (with a '
487	        'specific connection_to_question) may pass. Adjacent subject matter, generic filler, evidence for '
488	        'a different question, or bare metadata/methods fail.\n'
489	        '  - facet: is every facet tag supported by the span and a member of CONTRACT_FACETS?\n'
490	        '  - content_classes: give a content class for EVERY non-empty card field. Fail any field that is '
491	        'process narration, model self-talk, instructions, candidate enumeration, confidence commentary, '
492	        'or prompt/JSON scaffolding. Absence of suspicious phrases is not sufficient.\n'
493	        '  - proposed_disposition: one of the allowed dispositions.\n\n'
494	        'Echo audit_row_id exactly. Do not include any field not in the schema.\n\n'
495	        f'SCHEMA:\n{json.dumps(schema)}\n\n'
496	        f'PACKET:\n{json.dumps(packet, ensure_ascii=False)}\n'
497	    )

501	# One Opus pass — call the runner, validate, prove opus, parse the receipt
502	# =================================================================================================
503	@dataclass
504	class OpusVerdict:
505	    """One accepted, schema-valid Opus receipt (Sol Phase 3 step 6). It is NEVER the model's reasoning
506	    trace — only the structured decision."""
507	    audit_row_id: str
508	    pass_label: str                    # PASS_A / PASS_B / PASS_ADJ
509	    model_verified: bool
510	    verdict: dict                      # the validated response object
511	    cost_usd: float = 0.0
512	
513	    def to_json(self) -> dict:
514	        return dict(audit_row_id=self.audit_row_id, pass_label=self.pass_label,
515	                    model_verified=self.model_verified, verdict=self.verdict, cost_usd=self.cost_usd)
516	
517	    @property
518	    def disposition(self) -> str:
519	        return self.verdict.get('proposed_disposition', '')
520

522	def _extract_response_text(envelope: dict) -> str:
523	    """The `claude -p --output-format json` envelope carries the model's structured answer in `result`."""
524	    return envelope.get('result') if isinstance(envelope.get('result'), str) else json.dumps(
525	        envelope.get('result') or {})

528	def run_opus_pass(card: dict, graph: P.Graph, *, question: str, contract_facets: list[str],
529	                  det_receipt, pass_label: str, runner, support_role: str = 'primary') -> OpusVerdict:
530	    """Run ONE independent Opus pass on one card. `runner` is the injectable transport (Sol: a fresh
531	    session; the two passes never share context). Validates the response against the schema, proves the
532	    model was Opus, and refuses anything malformed — a missing/uncertain/garbage response is a FAILURE,
533	    never a silent pass (Sol Phase 1/3)."""
534	    schema = opus_response_json_schema()
535	    packet = build_opus_packet(card, graph, question=question, contract_facets=contract_facets,
536	                               det_receipt=det_receipt, support_role=support_role)
537	    prompt = build_opus_prompt(packet, schema)
538	    envelope = runner(prompt, schema)                   # may raise OpusUnavailable — caller fails closed
539	    verified = model_is_opus(envelope)
540	    text = _extract_response_text(envelope)
541	    m = re.search(r'\{.*\}', text or '', re.S)
542	    try:
543	        obj = json.loads(m.group(0)) if m else json.loads(text)
544	    except Exception as e:                              # noqa: BLE001
545	        raise OpusResponseInvalid(f'response was not parseable JSON: {type(e).__name__}')
546	    validated = validate_opus_response(obj, det_receipt.audit_row_id)
547	    return OpusVerdict(det_receipt.audit_row_id, pass_label, verified, validated,
548	                       float(envelope.get('total_cost_usd') or 0.0))

554	@dataclass
555	class CombinedVerdict:
556	    """The join of Tier-0, report-AST faithfulness, and the two Opus passes for one card (Sol Phase 4
557	    step 1). `needs_adjudication` routes to Tier 3; `final` is only PASS when EVERY applicable dimension
558	    passes after any repair (Sol §2: no UNCERTAIN reaches the composer)."""
559	    audit_row_id: str
560	    final: str                         # PASS / FAIL / UNCERTAIN
561	    needs_adjudication: bool
562	    proposed_disposition: str
563	    reason_codes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
564	    detail: str = ''

567	        return dict(audit_row_id=self.audit_row_id, final=self.final,
568	                    needs_adjudication=self.needs_adjudication,
569	                    proposed_disposition=self.proposed_disposition,
570	                    reason_codes=sorted(set(self.reason_codes)), detail=self.detail)

573	def _opus_dimension_fail(v: dict) -> list[str]:
574	    """Which semantic dimensions this Opus verdict says fail (Sol §Relevance/§Numeric/§CoT/§Facet)."""
575	    rcs: list[str] = []
576	    rel = v.get('relevance') or {}
577	    if rel.get('verdict') != PASS or rel.get('classification') not in REL_PASS_CLASSES:
578	        rcs.append(RC_OPUS_RELEVANCE)
579	    num = v.get('numeric') or {}
580	    if num.get('applicable') and any(x == FAIL for x in (num.get('subdimensions') or {}).values()):
581	        rcs.append(RC_OPUS_NUMERIC)
582	    if (v.get('facet') or {}).get('verdict') == FAIL:
583	        rcs.append(RC_OPUS_FACET)
584	    if any(cc.get('verdict') == FAIL for cc in (v.get('content_classes') or [])):
585	        rcs.append(RC_OPUS_COT)
586	    return rcs

589	def combine_card_verdicts(det_receipt, faith: FaithfulnessReceipt,
590	                          corr_faith: list[FaithfulnessReceipt],
591	                          opus_a: OpusVerdict | None, opus_b: OpusVerdict | None) -> CombinedVerdict:
592	    """Fail-closed join. Authority order (Sol §Faithfulness, §Tier 3):
593	       1. A Tier-0 deterministic FAIL (structure/binding/caches) => card FAIL, quarantine.
594	       2. A report-AST faithfulness FAIL => card FAIL; it CANNOT be overridden by Opus.
595	       3. Both Opus passes must be present, model-verified, and agree; otherwise adjudicate.
596	       4. report-AST PASS but Opus alleges an unsupported faithfulness atom => adjudicate (do NOT silently
597	          ship, but do NOT let an Opus opinion override the byte-level PASS either).
598	       5. Any Opus semantic FAIL (relevance/numeric/facet/CoT) => adjudicate/repair, not KEEP."""
599	    rid = det_receipt.audit_row_id
600	    rcs: list[str] = []
601	
602	    if det_receipt.overall == FAIL:
603	        for d in det_receipt.dimensions.values():
604	            rcs.extend(d.reason_codes)
605	        return CombinedVerdict(rid, FAIL, False, 'QUARANTINE_CARD', rcs,
606	                               'Tier-0 deterministic failure is decisive')
607	
608	    if faith.verdict == FAIL:
609	        return CombinedVerdict(rid, FAIL, True, 'QUARANTINE_CARD', list(faith.reason_codes),
610	                               'report-AST faithfulness FAIL cannot be overridden by Opus')
611	    bad_corr = [c for c in corr_faith if c.verdict == FAIL]
612	    if bad_corr:
613	        rcs.extend(rc for c in bad_corr for rc in c.reason_codes)
614	        return CombinedVerdict(rid, FAIL, True, 'QUARANTINE_SUPPORT_EDGE', rcs,
615	                               f'{len(bad_corr)} corroborator(s) do not independently entail the primary')
616	
617	    if opus_a is None or opus_b is None:
618	        return CombinedVerdict(rid, UNCERTAIN, True, 'QUARANTINE_CARD', [RC_OPUS_TRANSPORT],
619	                               'a required independent Opus pass is missing — fail closed')
620	    if not (opus_a.model_verified and opus_b.model_verified):
621	        return CombinedVerdict(rid, UNCERTAIN, True, 'QUARANTINE_CARD', [RC_OPUS_MODEL],
622	                               'response did not prove the Opus model was used')
623	
624	    a_fail = _opus_dimension_fail(opus_a.verdict)
625	    b_fail = _opus_dimension_fail(opus_b.verdict)
626	    a_faith_fail = opus_a.verdict['faithfulness'].get('verdict') == FAIL
627	    b_faith_fail = opus_b.verdict['faithfulness'].get('verdict') == FAIL
628	
629	    # report-AST PASSED faithfulness but an Opus pass alleges an unsupported atom -> Tier 3 (Sol §Tier 3)
630	    if a_faith_fail or b_faith_fail:
631	        return CombinedVerdict(rid, UNCERTAIN, True, opus_a.disposition or 'QUARANTINE_CARD',
632	                               [RC_OPUS_ALLEGES_ATOM],
633	                               'report-AST passed but Opus alleges an unsupported faithfulness atom')
634	
635	    if set(a_fail) != set(b_fail) or opus_a.disposition != opus_b.disposition:
636	        return CombinedVerdict(rid, UNCERTAIN, True, opus_a.disposition or 'QUARANTINE_CARD',
637	                               sorted(set(a_fail + b_fail + [RC_OPUS_DISAGREE])),
638	                               'Opus passes disagree — adjudicate')
639	
640	    if a_fail:                                          # both passes agree on a semantic failure
641	        return CombinedVerdict(rid, FAIL, True, opus_a.disposition or 'REPAIR_TIGHTEN', sorted(set(a_fail)),
642	                               'both Opus passes agree the card fails a semantic dimension')
643	
644	    return CombinedVerdict(rid, PASS, False, 'KEEP_UNCHANGED', [], 'all applicable dimensions pass')
645	
646	
647	# =================================================================================================
648	# Tier 3 adjudication (Sol §Tier 3) — resolve a disagreement or an alleged false positive
649	# =================================================================================================
650	def build_adjudication_packet(card: dict, graph: P.Graph, det_receipt, faith: FaithfulnessReceipt,
651	                              opus_a: OpusVerdict, opus_b: OpusVerdict, *, question: str,
652	                              contract_facets: list[str]) -> dict:
653	    """Sol §Tier 3: the adjudicator receives the card, exact source bytes, deterministic receipt,
654	    report-AST result, and BOTH independent Opus verdicts. It must identify the deciding source substring
655	    and atom."""
656	    base = build_opus_packet(card, graph, question=question, contract_facets=contract_facets,
657	                             det_receipt=det_receipt)
658	    base['report_ast_faithfulness'] = faith.to_json()
659	    base['opus_pass_a'] = opus_a.to_json()
660	    base['opus_pass_b'] = opus_b.to_json()
661	    return base
662	
663	
664	def build_adjudication_prompt(packet: dict, schema: dict) -> str:
665	    return (
666	        'You are the ADJUDICATOR for ONE evidence card. You receive the card, its exact verified source '
667	        'bytes (RESOLVED_SPAN), the deterministic receipt, the report-AST faithfulness result, and two '
668	        'independent auditor verdicts (OPUS_PASS_A, OPUS_PASS_B). Decide the card, identifying the '
669	        'deciding source substring and the deciding atom. You MAY overturn an auditor FALSE POSITIVE. You '
670	        'may NOT override a failed byte binding or a failed report-AST entailment — if either failed, the '
671	        'card must be repaired and rerun, not passed. Return ONE JSON object matching the schema and '
672	        'nothing else. Echo audit_row_id exactly.\n\n'
673	        f'SCHEMA:\n{json.dumps(schema)}\n\n'
674	        f'PACKET:\n{json.dumps(packet, ensure_ascii=False)}\n'
675	    )
676	
677	
678	def run_adjudication(card: dict, graph: P.Graph, det_receipt, faith: FaithfulnessReceipt,
679	                     corr_faith: list[FaithfulnessReceipt], opus_a: OpusVerdict, opus_b: OpusVerdict, *,
680	                     question: str, contract_facets: list[str], runner) -> CombinedVerdict:
681	    """Sol §Tier 3: run the adjudicator and fold its verdict, still fail-closed. The adjudicator CANNOT
682	    resurrect a card whose byte binding or report-AST entailment failed — those routes return FAIL
683	    regardless of what the adjudicator says (Sol §Tier 3: 'it cannot override a failed byte binding or
684	    failed entailed_by_span')."""
685	    rid = det_receipt.audit_row_id
686	    # Hard floors the adjudicator may never lift.
687	    if det_receipt.overall == FAIL:
688	        return CombinedVerdict(rid, FAIL, False, 'QUARANTINE_CARD', ['tier0.deterministic_fail'],
689	                               'adjudicator cannot lift a Tier-0 deterministic failure')
690	    if faith.verdict == FAIL:
691	        return CombinedVerdict(rid, FAIL, False, 'QUARANTINE_CARD', list(faith.reason_codes),
692	                               'adjudicator cannot override a failed report-AST entailment')
693	
694	    schema = opus_response_json_schema()
695	    packet = build_adjudication_packet(card, graph, det_receipt, faith, opus_a, opus_b,
696	                                       question=question, contract_facets=contract_facets)
697	    prompt = build_adjudication_prompt(packet, schema)
698	    envelope = runner(prompt, schema)
699	    if not model_is_opus(envelope):
700	        return CombinedVerdict(rid, UNCERTAIN, False, 'QUARANTINE_CARD', [RC_OPUS_MODEL],
701	                               'adjudicator response did not prove the Opus model')
702	    text = _extract_response_text(envelope)
703	    m = re.search(r'\{.*\}', text or '', re.S)
704	    try:
705	        obj = json.loads(m.group(0)) if m else json.loads(text)
706	        validated = validate_opus_response(obj, rid)
707	    except Exception as e:                              # noqa: BLE001
708	        return CombinedVerdict(rid, UNCERTAIN, False, 'QUARANTINE_CARD', [RC_OPUS_SCHEMA],
709	                               f'adjudicator response invalid: {type(e).__name__}')
710	    adj = OpusVerdict(rid, PASS_ADJ, True, validated, float(envelope.get('total_cost_usd') or 0.0))
711	    disp = adj.disposition
712	    sem_fail = _opus_dimension_fail(adj.verdict) or adj.verdict['faithfulness'].get('verdict') == FAIL
713	    # Sol §Voice: block the one illegal ATTRIBUTED->OWNED laundering transition.
714	    if voice_launder_blocked(disp, faith):
715	        return CombinedVerdict(rid, FAIL, False, 'QUARANTINE_CARD', [RC_VOICE_LAUNDER],
716	                               'OWNED demotion of an unreachable card blocked — no source bytes to demote')
717	    if disp == 'KEEP_UNCHANGED' and not sem_fail:
718	        return CombinedVerdict(rid, PASS, False, 'KEEP_UNCHANGED', [], 'adjudicator resolved to keep')
719	    final = FAIL if disp in ('QUARANTINE_CARD', 'QUARANTINE_SUPPORT_EDGE') else UNCERTAIN
720	    return CombinedVerdict(rid, final, False, disp,
721	                           (_opus_dimension_fail(adj.verdict) or ['adjudicator.repair_or_quarantine']),
722	                           'adjudicator disposition')
723	
724	
725	# =================================================================================================
726	# End-to-end audit of one card (Sol §7 production sequence, per-card slice) — runner injected
727	# =================================================================================================
728	def audit_card(card: dict, graph: P.Graph, det_receipt, *, question: str, contract_facets: list[str],
729	               runner) -> CombinedVerdict:
730	    """Run the full semantic ladder on ONE card with an injected Opus transport: report-AST faithfulness
731	    (authoritative), Tier-1 pass A, Tier-2 pass B, then Tier-3 adjudication when the join requires it.
732	    A transport failure fails CLOSED (UNCERTAIN + quarantine), never a silent pass."""
733	    faith = audit_faithfulness_primary(card, graph)
734	    corr = audit_faithfulness_corroborators(card, graph)
735	
736	    def _pass(label):
737	        try:
738	            return run_opus_pass(card, graph, question=question, contract_facets=contract_facets,
739	                                 det_receipt=det_receipt, pass_label=label, runner=runner)
740	        except (OpusUnavailable, OpusResponseInvalid):
741	            return None
742	
743	    opus_a = _pass(PASS_A)
744	    opus_b = _pass(PASS_B)
745	    combined = combine_card_verdicts(det_receipt, faith, corr, opus_a, opus_b)
746	    if combined.needs_adjudication and opus_a is not None and opus_b is not None \
747	            and det_receipt.overall != FAIL and faith.verdict != FAIL:
748	        try:
749	            return run_adjudication(card, graph, det_receipt, faith, corr, opus_a, opus_b,
750	                                    question=question, contract_facets=contract_facets, runner=runner)
751	        except OpusUnavailable:
752	            return CombinedVerdict(det_receipt.audit_row_id, UNCERTAIN, False, 'QUARANTINE_CARD',
753	                                   [RC_OPUS_TRANSPORT], 'adjudicator transport failed — fail closed')
754	    return combined

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Repoint the card-audit judge transport from `claude -p --model opus` to OpenRouter glm-5.2.
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS
5	    `card_audit.harness` was built for a `claude -p --model opus --effort max` transport
6	    (`subprocess_opus_runner`), which would take ~12-24h for 5,759 cards. The operator wants the
7	    SAME audit — same prompts, same dimensions, same disposition logic — pointed at the fast
8	    OpenRouter glm-5.2 model the miner already uses, run in parallel with the compose job.
9	
10	WHAT IS SWAPPED (only the transport, never the judgement):
11	  1. THE SEMANTIC RUNNER. `make_glm_runner()` returns a callable(prompt, schema) -> envelope that
12	     mirrors the `claude -p --output-format json` envelope harness expects (keys: result, modelUsage,
13	     model, is_error, total_cost_usd). It reuses `OpenRouterClient(model='z-ai/glm-5.2')` exactly as
14	     `evidence_miner.llm()` / `cellcog_composer.llm()` do, with a LARGE max_tokens (default 18000) so
15	     the reasoning-first model does not truncate its verdict mid-plan (the I-bug-089 lesson).
16	
17	  2. THE "PROVE IT WAS OPUS" GATE. `harness.model_is_opus` (and the combine step that reads
18	     `model_verified`) exists to reject a cheaper model masquerading as opus. We are INTENTIONALLY
19	     using glm-5.2, so `install_glm_model_gate()` monkeypatches `harness.model_is_opus` to instead
20	     prove the response came from the INTENDED judge model (glm-5.2) and was not silently downgraded
21	     to some OTHER model. It is a relaxation of the opus-specific check, not a removal of the
22	     "no silent downgrade" invariant.
23	
24	  3. THE ENTAILMENT (faithfulness) JUDGE. `report_ast.entailed_by_span` is authoritative and is NOT
25	     edited. Its default judge (`report_ast._llm_entailment_judge`) already calls the glm-5.2 model
26	     via `cellcog_composer.llm(prompt, max_tokens=300)` with the exact strict-entailment prompt — but
27	     300 tokens truncates a reasoning-first model before it can answer, which would fail EVERY card
28	     closed (a systemic false-negative). `install_glm_entailment_judge()` floors that token budget to
29	     a large value (I-bug-089) WITHOUT touching the prompt, then wraps the judge in the error-sentinel
30	     guard (`judge_guard.install_guarded_judge`). Prompt and semantics are untouched; only the token
31	     budget and model-proof are.
32	
33	Nothing here hardcodes a subject/DOI/venue — it is a pure transport shim.
34	"""
35	from __future__ import annotations
36	
37	import asyncio
38	import json
39	import threading
40	
41	JUDGE_MODEL = 'z-ai/glm-5.2'
42	# LARGE max_tokens so the reasoning-first judge does not truncate its structured verdict (I-bug-089).
43	SEMANTIC_MAX_TOKENS = 18000
44	# The entailment (faithfulness) judge asks for a tiny JSON, but glm-5.2 emits a reasoning prelude
45	# first; a 300-token budget starves it. Floor it well above the reasoning prelude.
46	ENTAILMENT_MIN_TOKENS = 16000
47	
48	
49	# =================================================================================================
50	# 1. The semantic runner — a claude-p-shaped envelope produced by an OpenRouter glm-5.2 call
51	# =================================================================================================
52	def _glm_generate(prompt: str, *, model: str, max_tokens: int, timeout_s: float):
53	    """One synchronous glm-5.2 call. Mirrors evidence_miner.llm(): a fresh OpenRouterClient in its own
54	    event loop (thread-safe — each ThreadPoolExecutor worker owns its loop). Returns the LLMResponse."""
55	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient
56	
57	    async def _run():
58	        c = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
59	        try:
60	            return await c.generate(prompt=prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens, temperature=0.0,
61	                                    timeout=timeout_s)
62	        finally:
63	            cl = getattr(c, 'close', None)
64	            if cl:
65	                try:
66	                    res = cl()
67	                    if hasattr(res, '__await__'):
68	                        await res
69	                except Exception:
70	                    pass
71	
72	    return asyncio.run(_run())
73	
74	
75	def make_glm_runner(*, model: str = JUDGE_MODEL, max_tokens: int = SEMANTIC_MAX_TOKENS,
76	                    timeout_s: float = 600.0, max_retries: int = 3):
77	    """Build the injectable OpusRunner replacement. Signature/return shape match
78	    `harness.subprocess_opus_runner`: callable(prompt, schema) -> the parsed `claude -p` JSON envelope
79	    (result / modelUsage / model / is_error / total_cost_usd). `schema` is accepted for signature
80	    compatibility (harness also embeds the schema in the prompt text); glm has no server-side
81	    json-schema mode here, so we rely on the prompt's schema instruction + harness's own validator."""
82	
83	    def runner(prompt: str, schema: dict) -> dict:
84	        last_err = ''
85	        for _attempt in range(max(1, max_retries)):
86	            try:
87	                r = _glm_generate(prompt, model=model, max_tokens=max_tokens, timeout_s=timeout_s)
88	            except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001 — a transport fault retries the SAME model, then fails closed
89	                last_err = f'{type(e).__name__}: {e}'
90	                continue
91	            content = getattr(r, 'content', None)
92	            if content is None and isinstance(r, dict):
93	                content = r.get('content')
94	            content = content if isinstance(content, str) else (str(r) if r is not None else '')
95	            if not content.strip():
96	                last_err = 'empty content from glm (possible truncation)'
97	                continue
98	            model_name = getattr(r, 'model', '') or model
99	            out_tok = int(getattr(r, 'output_tokens', 0) or 0) + int(getattr(r, 'reasoning_tokens', 0) or 0)
100	            in_tok = int(getattr(r, 'input_tokens', 0) or 0)
101	            # Envelope shaped like `claude -p --output-format json`, with modelUsage keyed by the model
102	            # id actually billed so the (patched) model gate can prove the intended judge answered.
103	            return {
104	                'result': content,
105	                'model': model_name,
106	                'modelUsage': {model_name: {'outputTokens': out_tok, 'inputTokens': in_tok}},
107	                'is_error': False,
108	                'total_cost_usd': 0.0,  # OpenRouterClient tracks cost in its own ledger; not needed here
109	            }
110	        # Fail closed exactly like the opus transport: raise OpusUnavailable so the caller quarantines.
111	        from card_audit import harness
112	        raise harness.OpusUnavailable(f'glm transport failed after {max_retries} retries: {last_err}')
113	
114	    return runner
115	
116	
117	# =================================================================================================
118	# 2. The model-proof gate — prove it was glm-5.2 (the intended judge), not a silent downgrade
119	# =================================================================================================
120	def install_glm_model_gate(model: str = JUDGE_MODEL) -> None:
121	    """Monkeypatch `harness.model_is_opus` so the audit accepts the INTENDED glm-5.2 judge instead of
122	    demanding opus. Still fail-closed against a silent downgrade: the substantive answer's dominant
123	    output-token model must be the intended judge family, and must not be some OTHER cheap model."""
124	    from card_audit import harness
125	
126	    family = model.split('/')[0].lower() if '/' in model else model.lower()
127	    short = model.split('/')[-1].lower()  # e.g. 'glm-5.2'
128	
129	    def model_is_intended_judge(result: dict) -> bool:
130	        usage = result.get('modelUsage') or {}
131	        hay = ' '.join(list(usage.keys()) + [str(result.get('model') or '')]).lower()
132	        # The intended judge must appear somewhere in the billed models / primary model.
133	        if short not in hay and family not in hay and model.lower() not in hay:
134	            return False
135	
136	        def _out(v):
137	            return (v.get('outputTokens') or v.get('output_tokens') or 0) if isinstance(v, dict) else 0
138	
139	        outs = {k.lower(): _out(v) for k, v in usage.items()}
140	        if outs and any(outs.values()):
141	            dominant = max(outs, key=lambda k: outs[k])
142	            return short in dominant or family in dominant or model.lower() in dominant
143	        primary = str(result.get('model') or '').lower()
144	        return short in primary or family in primary or model.lower() in primary
145	
146	    harness.model_is_opus = model_is_intended_judge  # type: ignore[assignment]
147	
148	
149	# =================================================================================================
150	# 3. The entailment (faithfulness) judge — same prompt, glm-5.2, LARGE token budget, guarded
151	# =================================================================================================
152	_orig_cellcog_llm = None
153	_llm_patch_lock = threading.Lock()
154	
155	
156	def install_glm_entailment_judge(min_tokens: int = ENTAILMENT_MIN_TOKENS) -> None:
157	    """Keep `report_ast._llm_entailment_judge` and its verbatim strict-entailment PROMPT, but ensure
158	    its glm-5.2 transport (`cellcog_composer.llm`) gets a LARGE token budget so the reasoning-first
159	    model does not truncate before emitting its JSON verdict (I-bug-089). Then wrap the judge in the
160	    error-sentinel guard so an ENTAILED-with-error reply is downgraded to UNCERTAIN (fail closed).
161	
162	    We do NOT edit report_ast.py or cellcog_composer.py on disk — this floors the token budget by
163	    wrapping `cellcog_composer.llm` in-process only."""
164	    global _orig_cellcog_llm
165	    import cellcog_composer as CC
166	    from card_audit import judge_guard
167	
168	    with _llm_patch_lock:
169	        if _orig_cellcog_llm is None:
170	            _orig_cellcog_llm = CC.llm
171	
172	            def llm_large_tokens(prompt: str, max_tokens: int = 8192) -> str:
173	                return _orig_cellcog_llm(prompt, max_tokens=max(max_tokens, min_tokens))
174	
175	            CC.llm = llm_large_tokens  # type: ignore[assignment]
176	
177	    # Wrap whatever entailment judge is currently wired (default: _llm_entailment_judge) in the guard.
178	    judge_guard.install_guarded_judge()
179	
180	
181	def install_all() -> dict:
182	    """Install every glm-5.2 transport swap and return a small manifest for the run report."""
183	    install_glm_model_gate(JUDGE_MODEL)
184	    install_glm_entailment_judge(ENTAILMENT_MIN_TOKENS)
185	    return {
186	        'judge_model': JUDGE_MODEL,
187	        'semantic_max_tokens': SEMANTIC_MAX_TOKENS,
188	        'entailment_min_tokens': ENTAILMENT_MIN_TOKENS,
189	        'model_gate': 'harness.model_is_opus -> prove intended glm-5.2 (no silent downgrade)',
190	        'entailment_judge': 'report_ast default judge (prompt intact) + large token floor + error-sentinel guard',
191	    }

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""THE ORCHESTRATOR: run the full evidence-card quality audit on every card in
3	outputs/evidence_cards_full.json using the glm-5.2 transport (card_audit.glm_transport), concurrently
4	and resumably, then write the audited card set and the audit report.
5	
6	PIPELINE PER CARD (Sol §7 production sequence, unchanged — only the JUDGE transport is glm-5.2 now):
7	  1. Tier-0 deterministic screen (card_audit.tier0.screen_card) — structure, binding, caches, numeric,
8	     CoT-contamination, facet, corroborator. Provable OFFLINE; no model call.
9	  2. report-AST faithfulness (harness.audit_faithfulness_primary / _corroborators) — the AUTHORITATIVE
10	     entailment verdict via report_ast.entailed_by_span (glm-5.2 judge, prompt intact, large tokens).
11	  3. glm-5.2 semantic ladder (harness.audit_card: Tier-1 pass A, Tier-2 pass B, Tier-3 adjudication) —
12	     faithfulness opinion, numeric fidelity, relevance, CoT content-class, facet. SHORT-CIRCUITED when
13	     Tier-0 or report-AST already fails the card (the two big passes would be ignored by the fail-closed
14	     join anyway), so an already-doomed card costs one cheap faithfulness call, not three big ones.
15	  4. Disposition engine (disposition.dispose_card) — KEEP / REPAIR / DEMOTE / QUARANTINE, fail-closed,
16	     fully accounted. Every quarantine/demote carries a reason code.
17	
18	RESUMABLE: each finished card is appended to a checkpoint JSONL keyed by audit_row_id; a re-run skips
19	rows already present. CONCURRENCY: a moderate ThreadPoolExecutor (default 9 workers) so the OpenRouter
20	key is shared with the concurrent compose job, never starved.
21	
22	READ-ONLY on the inputs: evidence_cards_full.json, provenance_graph.json, blobs, and the ledger are
23	never written. Writes only the checkpoint, evidence_cards_audited.json, and card_audit_report.md.
24	"""

26	
27	import argparse
28	import collections
29	import json
30	import os
31	import sys
32	import threading
33	import time

39	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]

45	from card_audit import disposition as D                            # noqa: E402
46	from card_audit.audit_schema import (  # noqa: E402
47	    PASS, FAIL, UNCERTAIN, NOT_APPLICABLE,

61	# Tier-0 deterministic dimensions we report pass-rates for (canonical audit_schema keys).
62	TIER0_DIMS = (DIM_STRUCTURE, DIM_BINDING, DIM_CACHES, DIM_NUMERIC, DIM_COT, DIM_FACET, DIM_CORROBORATOR)
63	# Semantic reason codes -> the dimension they indict (for per-dimension semantic fail tallies).

73	
74	def _policy_for(name: str) -> P.SourcePolicy:
75	    for pol in (P.JOURNAL_ONLY, P.PEER_REVIEWED, P.OFFICIAL_TEXT, P.ANY_VERSION):
76	        if pol.name == name:

80	
81	def _load_done_rids() -> set[str]:
82	    done: set[str] = set()

95	
96	def audit_one(idx: int, card: dict, graph: P.Graph, policy: P.SourcePolicy, question: str,
97	              dup_ids: frozenset, existing_ids: frozenset, runner) -> dict:
98	    """Run the whole pipeline on one card; return a checkpoint row (JSON-serializable)."""
99	    facets = list(card.get('facet_tags') or card.get('facet_tags_span') or [])
100	    det = tier0.screen_card(card, graph, policy, json_pointer=f'/{idx}', dup_ids=dup_ids)
101	    faith = harness.audit_faithfulness_primary(card, graph)
102	    corr = harness.audit_faithfulness_corroborators(card, graph)
103	
104	    reached_semantic = False
105	    if det.overall == FAIL or faith.verdict == FAIL:
106	        # ONLY these two authoritative floors let us skip the model: a Tier-0 deterministic FAIL or a
107	        # report-AST PRIMARY faithfulness FAIL make combine_card_verdicts return FAIL before it ever
108	        # consults the two Opus passes (harness authority order 1-2), so running them would be pure
109	        # waste. A doomed card then costs one cheap faithfulness call, not three big glm calls.
110	        #
111	        # A FAILING CORROBORATOR is deliberately NOT short-circuited here: when the PRIMARY is valid,
112	        # the full ladder (audit_card) runs Tier-1/2 and then Tier-3 adjudication, which can propose
113	        # REMOVE_BAD_SUPPORT_EDGE — keeping the valid primary and quarantining only the bad edge —

201	                for fut in as_completed(futs):

178	def _capture_reasoning_trace(
179	    result: "LLMResponse",
180	    content: str,
181	    reasoning: Optional[str],
182	    *,
183	    status: str = "ok",
184	) -> None:
185	    """Record one raw completed provider response to the run-scoped
186	    reasoning-trace sink, if one is registered AND the caller set a generator
187	    call-context. ``content_source`` is recorded as ``direct`` here;
188	    generate()/reason() finalize it (promoted_from_reasoning /
189	    extracted_from_reasoning) via the sink's update() using
190	    ``result.trace_call_id``. Best-effort observability — a sink failure is
191	    logged loud but never breaks the LLM call (reasoning trace is process
192	    transparency, not the generation path)."""
193	    sink = current_reasoning_sink()
194	    ctx = current_reasoning_call_context()
195	    if sink is None or not ctx:
196	        return
197	    try:
198	        result.trace_call_id = sink.record(
199	            section=str(ctx.get("section", "")),
200	            call_type=str(ctx.get("call_type", "section")),
201	            model=result.model or "",
202	            status=status,
203	            content_source="direct",
204	            parent_call_id=ctx.get("parent_call_id"),
205	            regen_reason=ctx.get("regen_reason"),
206	            attempt_n=int(ctx.get("attempt_n", 1) or 1),
207	            reasoning_text=reasoning or "",
208	            content_text=content or "",
209	            input_tokens=result.input_tokens,
210	            output_tokens=result.output_tokens,
211	            reasoning_tokens=result.reasoning_tokens,
212	        )
213	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — observability must not break generation
214	        logger.warning(
215	            "[polaris graph] I-gen-004: reasoning-trace capture failed "
216	            "(%s: %s) — generation continues, this record dropped",
217	            type(exc).__name__,
218	            exc,
219	        )

222	def _finalize_reasoning_trace(call_id: Optional[str], **patch: object) -> None:
223	    """I-gen-004 (#496): patch an already-captured reasoning-trace record once
224	    the caller (generate()/reason()) has resolved promotion / `</think>`
225	    extraction / retry / truncation — e.g. content_source=promoted_from_reasoning
226	    or status=retry|truncated|error. No-op if no sink or no call_id.
227	    Best-effort: a sink failure is logged, never raised."""
228	    if not call_id:

231	    if sink is None:

237	            "[polaris graph] I-gen-004: reasoning-trace finalize failed "
238	            "(%s: %s) — generation continues",
239	            type(exc).__name__,
240	            exc,
241	        )

308	# FX-11 (I-ready-017 BUG-10/10b): a PROCESS-GLOBAL, lock-protected, per-SESSION monotonic
309	# accumulator for the cost LEDGER's `cumulative_cost_usd`. The budget guard uses the per-task
310	# ContextVar `current_run_cost()`, which is RESET inside each parallel four-role claim worker
311	# (sweep_integration) and merged to the parent only at the end — so a per-row cumulative read
312	# from it is per-worker and NON-monotonic/interleaved. The ledger's cumulative must instead be a
313	# single rising run total regardless of which thread/worker writes the row (Uptrace SOTA: LLM
314	# cost is a MONOTONIC counter). Keyed by session_id (run_id, unique per run) so concurrent
315	# workers of ONE run share the counter and distinct runs in one process never bleed. The budget
316	# GATE (current_run_cost / check_run_budget) is intentionally NOT changed.
317	# RLock (reentrant): append_cost_ledger_row holds it across bump+write and calls
318	# ledger_bump_cumulative, which re-acquires it. A plain Lock would self-deadlock.
319	_LEDGER_CUM_LOCK = threading.RLock()
320	_LEDGER_CUM_BY_SESSION: dict[str, float] = {}
321	
322	
323	def ledger_bump_cumulative(session_id: str, cost: float) -> float:
324	    """Atomically add `cost` to the per-session ledger total; return the new total (rounded 4).
325	
326	    Monotonic within a `session_id` across threads/workers — this is the value every cost-ledger
327	    row must record as `cumulative_cost_usd`.
328	    """
329	    sid = session_id or "no_run_id"
330	    with _LEDGER_CUM_LOCK:
331	        total = _LEDGER_CUM_BY_SESSION.get(sid, 0.0) + float(cost)
332	        _LEDGER_CUM_BY_SESSION[sid] = total
333	        return round(total, 4)
334	
335	
336	def reset_ledger_cumulative(session_id: str) -> None:
337	    """Clear the per-session ledger accumulator so a re-used `session_id` in one process starts
338	    fresh. Production run_ids are unique per run (no reset needed); tests reuse ids and call this.
339	    NOTE: do NOT hook this into reset_run_cost — that is called per parallel worker and would zero
340	    the shared run total mid-run.
341	    """
342	    sid = session_id or "no_run_id"
343	    with _LEDGER_CUM_LOCK:
344	        _LEDGER_CUM_BY_SESSION.pop(sid, None)
345	
346	
347	def ledger_cumulative(session_id: str) -> float:
348	    """Read the per-session monotonic ledger total (process-global, thread-safe). I-arch-002 (#1251):
349	    when a spend-bearing pass runs OFF the event-loop thread via ``asyncio.to_thread`` (the credibility
350	    judging pool), its cost lands in this accumulator (every ``append_cost_ledger_row`` bumps it under the
351	    lock) but NOT in the event-loop task's ``_RUN_COST_CTX`` (a copied context). The caller reconciles by
352	    reading this before/after the offload and ``_add_run_cost(after - before)`` so the run-budget gate stays
353	    inclusive. Returns 0.0 for an unseen session."""
354	    sid = session_id or "no_run_id"
355	    with _LEDGER_CUM_LOCK:
356	        return _LEDGER_CUM_BY_SESSION.get(sid, 0.0)
357	
358	
359	def append_cost_ledger_row(
360	    *,
361	    session_id: str,
362	    call_type: str,
363	    cost_usd: float,
364	    input_tokens: int = 0,
365	    output_tokens: int = 0,
366	    reasoning_tokens: int = 0,
367	    duration_ms: float = 0.0,
368	) -> float:
369	    """THE single canonical cost-ledger writer (FX-11, I-ready-017 #1116 — issue title).
370	
371	    Under ONE re-entrant process-global lock: (1) add ``cost_usd`` to the per-session monotonic
372	    accumulator, (2) append the row — carrying that inclusive ``cumulative_cost_usd`` — to the
373	    JSONL ledger. Bumping and appending share the lock so the PERSISTED FILE is non-decreasing in
374	    WRITE order (not merely in assignment order) even under the parallel four-role
375	    ThreadPoolExecutor workers (sweep_integration), which each copy_context() (inheriting the run
376	    id) and reset their own ``_RUN_COST_CTX``. Returns the new cumulative (rounded 4).
377	
378	    Best-effort I/O: a write failure is logged, NEVER raised, and the in-memory accumulator still
379	    advances so it stays == the run-budget total. Used by the four-role role / judge / blank-retry
380	    writers; the generator (UsageTracker.record) bumps the SAME accumulator under the SAME lock via
381	    its ``_append_ledger`` hook (kept distinct only because a test monkeypatches that hook).
382	    """
383	    sid = session_id or "no_run_id"
384	    with _LEDGER_CUM_LOCK:
385	        total = ledger_bump_cumulative(sid, cost_usd)  # reentrant acquire (RLock)
386	        row = {
387	            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
388	            "session_id": sid,
389	            "call_type": call_type,
390	            "input_tokens": int(input_tokens),
391	            "output_tokens": int(output_tokens),
392	            "reasoning_tokens": int(reasoning_tokens),
393	            "duration_ms": round(float(duration_ms), 1),
394	            "cost_usd": round(float(cost_usd), 6),
395	            "cumulative_cost_usd": total,
396	        }
397	        try:
398	            _COST_LEDGER_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
399	            with open(_COST_LEDGER_PATH, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
400	                f.write(json.dumps(row) + "\n")
401	        except Exception as exc:  # best-effort: never break the caller's call/budget path
402	            logger.warning("FIX-O1/FX-11: Cost ledger write failed: %s", exc)
403	        return total

444	def _impute_cost_from_tokens(
445	    model: str,
446	    input_tokens: int,
447	    output_tokens: int,
448	    reasoning_tokens: int,
449	) -> float:
450	    """Estimate $/call from token counts + published prices. Reasoning
451	    tokens bill at the output rate (matches OpenAI / Anthropic practice).
452	    Used by the budget guard when OpenRouter omits usage.cost.
453	
454	    Defensive: token counts are clamped to >=0. A corrupted API response
455	    returning negative tokens must NOT produce a negative cost that
456	    would silently shrink the accumulated run budget and let a runaway
457	    loop keep calling past PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN.
458	    """
459	    # Clamp negative token counts — these can only come from a
460	    # corrupted API response. A negative cost here would be silently
461	    # absorbed by _add_run_cost and weaken the budget guard.
462	    input_tokens = max(0, int(input_tokens))
463	    output_tokens = max(0, int(output_tokens))
464	    reasoning_tokens = max(0, int(reasoning_tokens))
465	    if input_tokens == 0 and output_tokens == 0 and reasoning_tokens == 0:
466	        return 0.0
467	    model_lower = (model or "").lower()
468	    input_rate, output_rate = _DEFAULT_PRICE_PER_M
469	    for prefix, (in_r, out_r) in _PRICE_TABLE_USD_PER_M.items():
470	        if model_lower.startswith(prefix):
471	            input_rate, output_rate = in_r, out_r
472	            break
473	    return (
474	        (input_tokens / 1_000_000.0) * input_rate
475	        + ((output_tokens + reasoning_tokens) / 1_000_000.0) * output_rate
476	    )

479	def check_run_budget(anticipated_additional: float = 0.0) -> None:
480	    """Raise BudgetExceededError if the next call would exceed the cap.
481	
482	    anticipated_additional: optional estimate of the upcoming call cost
483	    (used only for guard-before-call — the actual cost is recorded by
484	    `_add_run_cost()` after the call returns).
485	    """
486	    current = current_run_cost()
487	    projected = current + max(0.0, float(anticipated_additional))
488	    if projected > PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN:
489	        raise BudgetExceededError(
490	            f"PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN={PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN:.4f} exceeded: "
491	            f"current=${current:.4f} + anticipated=${anticipated_additional:.4f} "
492	            f"= ${projected:.4f}. Set PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN higher or call "
493	            f"reset_run_cost() between runs."
494	        )

655	def family_from_model(model_name: str, override: str = "") -> str:
656	    """Derive the training-lineage family from an OpenRouter model name.
657	
658	    Returns the family label ("deepseek", "qwen", "glm", "llama", "gemma",
659	    "mistral", "kimi", "openai", "anthropic", "google-closed") or
660	    "unknown" if no prefix matches.
661	
662	    A non-empty `override` bypasses prefix matching. Use it when a model
663	    has been renamed or when a fine-tune needs its base-family tag.
664	    """
665	    if override:
666	        return override.strip().lower()
667	    if not model_name:
668	        return "unknown"
669	    m = model_name.strip().lower()
670	    for family, prefixes in _FAMILY_PREFIXES.items():
671	        for prefix in prefixes:
672	            if m.startswith(prefix.lower()):
673	                return family
674	    return "unknown"
