677	def check_family_segregation(
678	    generator_model: str | None = None,
679	    evaluator_model: str | None = None,
680	    generator_override: str | None = None,
681	    evaluator_override: str | None = None,
682	) -> tuple[str, str]:
683	    """Fail fast if generator and evaluator are in the same family.
684	
685	    Returns (generator_family, evaluator_family) on success.
686	    Raises RuntimeError if same family or if either is "unknown" without
687	    an explicit override (so misconfiguration is visible at init time).
688	
689	    Defaults pull from the module-level env-var settings.
690	    """
691	    gen_model = generator_model or PG_GENERATOR_MODEL
692	    eval_model = evaluator_model or PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL
693	    gen_override = generator_override if generator_override is not None else PG_GENERATOR_FAMILY_OVERRIDE
694	    eval_override = evaluator_override if evaluator_override is not None else PG_EVALUATOR_FAMILY_OVERRIDE
695	
696	    gen_family = family_from_model(gen_model, gen_override)
697	    eval_family = family_from_model(eval_model, eval_override)
698	
699	    if gen_family == "unknown" and not gen_override:
700	        raise RuntimeError(
701	            f"HONEST-REBUILD Phase 1c: generator model '{gen_model}' does not "
702	            f"match any known family prefix. Set PG_GENERATOR_FAMILY_OVERRIDE "
703	            f"to the training-lineage family (e.g. 'deepseek', 'qwen') so "
704	            f"family segregation can be checked. Family-distance from the "
705	            f"evaluator is the primary mitigation for evaluator-generator "
706	            f"self-bias (Play Favorites arXiv:2508.06709, DeepHalluBench "
707	            f"arXiv:2601.22984)."
708	        )
709	    if eval_family == "unknown" and not eval_override:
710	        raise RuntimeError(
711	            f"HONEST-REBUILD Phase 1c: evaluator model '{eval_model}' does "
712	            f"not match any known family prefix. Set PG_EVALUATOR_FAMILY_OVERRIDE."
713	        )
714	    if gen_family == eval_family and os.getenv(
715	        "PG_PERMIT_GENERATOR_EVALUATOR_SAME_FAMILY", "0"
716	    ).strip() in ("1", "true", "True"):
717	        # I-beatboth-008 (#1285, operator-approved 2026-06-20): all-GLM-5.2 two-family relaxation.
718	        # The independent-lineage self-bias safeguard is DELIBERATELY disabled for this run (disclosed
719	        # LOUD, never silent). The deterministic strict_verify floor + minimax Sentinel + qwen Judge D8
720	        # roles remain independent. Default-OFF: unset => the RuntimeError below still fires (crown-jewel
721	        # two-family invariant preserved for every non-campaign caller + the crown-jewel test).
722	        logger.warning(
723	            "[polaris graph] I-beatboth-008 #1285: generator and evaluator are BOTH family %r and "
724	            "PG_PERMIT_GENERATOR_EVALUATOR_SAME_FAMILY=1 -> two-family self-bias safeguard DISABLED "
725	            "for this run (operator-approved all-GLM-5.2). Disclosed.", gen_family,
726	        )
727	        return (gen_family, eval_family)
728	    if gen_family == eval_family:
729	        raise RuntimeError(
730	            f"HONEST-REBUILD Phase 1c: generator and evaluator are in the "
731	            f"same training-lineage family ('{gen_family}'). This defeats "
732	            f"the purpose of two-family architecture — same-family judges "
733	            f"share blind spots and RLHF biases. Pick models from different "
734	            f"families, or document the choice with explicit PG_*_FAMILY_OVERRIDE "
735	            f"values to demonstrate you know the trade-off. Recommended pair: "
736	            f"deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro (generator) + google/gemma-4-31b-it "
737	            f"(evaluator) per loopback/audit/_open_source_models_2026.md."
738	        )
739	    return (gen_family, eval_family)

742	def validate_role_families(role_map: dict[str, str], policy: str = "all_distinct",
743	                           allowed_collisions: list[tuple[str, str]] | None = None) -> dict[str, str]:
744	    """I-meta-001 (#933): N-way family segregation for the 4-role architecture.
745	
746	    Extends the pairwise check_family_segregation to N roles per the locked
747	    architecture (Generator + Mirror + Sentinel + Judge). All four roles must
748	    be from distinct training lineages by default; allowed_collisions overrides
749	    specific pairs if the architecture lock's family_policy permits them.
750	
751	    Args:
752	        role_map: {role_name: model_slug}, e.g. {"generator": "deepseek/...",
753	                  "mirror": "cohere/...", "sentinel": "ibm-granite/...",
754	                  "judge": "qwen/..."}
755	        policy: "all_distinct" (default) or "permit_collisions".
756	        allowed_collisions: list of [role_a, role_b] pairs whose families
757	                  may collide. Ignored under all_distinct.
758	
759	    Returns:
760	        {role_name: family_label} on success.
761	
762	    Raises:
763	        RuntimeError if any role's family is "unknown" or if two roles share
764	        a family outside allowed_collisions.
765	
766	    Codex APPROVE iter 2 — accept_remaining (see .codex/I-meta-001/codex_brief_iter2_verdict.txt).
767	    """
768	    allowed_collisions = allowed_collisions or []
769	    role_families: dict[str, str] = {}
770	    for role, model in role_map.items():
771	        family = family_from_model(model)
772	        if family == "unknown":
773	            raise RuntimeError(
774	                f"validate_role_families: role {role!r} model {model!r} returns "
775	                f"family='unknown'. Add the family prefix to "
776	                f"_FAMILY_PREFIXES in openrouter_client.py."
777	            )
778	        role_families[role] = family
779	
780	    if policy == "permit_collisions":
781	        return role_families
782	
783	    # all_distinct (default policy): every family must be unique across roles
784	    family_to_role: dict[str, str] = {}
785	    allowed_set = {tuple(sorted([a, b])) for a, b in allowed_collisions}
786	    for role, family in role_families.items():
787	        if family in family_to_role:
788	            other_role = family_to_role[family]
789	            if tuple(sorted([role, other_role])) in allowed_set:
790	                continue
791	            raise RuntimeError(
792	                f"validate_role_families: family {family!r} appears in both "
793	                f"role {role!r} and role {other_role!r}; family_policy="
794	                f"all_distinct forbids this. If intentional, add the pair to "
795	                f"allowed_collisions in config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml."
796	            )
797	        family_to_role[family] = role
798	    return role_families

899	def _resolve_call_timeout(
900	    model: str,
901	    explicit: Optional[float],
902	    nonreasoning_floor: float,
903	) -> float:
904	    """I-arch-004 F20 (#1255): size a per-call LLM timeout off the GENERATOR budget for
905	    reasoning-first models, instead of a small per-method default that SHADOWS it.
906	
907	    The bug this closes: ``reason()`` capped at ``LONG_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`` (180s) and
908	    ``generate_structured()`` at ``DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`` (90s) via a ``timeout or <small>``
909	    fallback. A reasoning-first model (DeepSeek V4 Pro, GLM-5.1, MiniMax-M2 — :data:`_REASONING_FIRST_MODELS`)
910	    needs MINUTES per call; the small per-method clock killed the call long before the model
911	    finished reasoning -> truncated/empty content. ``generate()`` already passes ``None`` through
912	    so ``_call_impl`` resolves :data:`GENERATOR_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`; this helper gives ``reason()`` /
913	    ``generate_structured()`` the SAME generous derivation WITHOUT regressing the non-reasoning
914	    floor (each method keeps its own ``nonreasoning_floor`` — LONG for reason, DEFAULT for
915	    structured) for the verifier / judge / side-judge calls that also flow through them.
916	
917	    Resolution order (CLAUDE.md §9.1.8 — never starve a reasoning-first call):
918	      1. An EXPLICIT caller timeout always wins (caller knows best; LAW VI override).
919	      2. Reasoning-first model + no explicit timeout -> the LIVE generator budget
920	         (``get_generator_timeout_seconds()`` so the Gate-B slate's
921	         ``set_generator_timeout_seconds()`` is honored, NOT a value frozen at import).
922	      3. Otherwise (non-reasoning-first, no explicit) -> the method's existing floor
923	         -> byte-identical to the pre-F20 behavior for those calls.
924	
925	    The $ hard cap (``PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN``), NOT the clock, is the spend backstop.
926	    """
927	    if explicit is not None:
928	        return explicit
929	    if model in _REASONING_FIRST_MODELS:
930	        return get_generator_timeout_seconds()
931	    return nonreasoning_floor

(Note: your "175/5759" is stale; the log reached 350 and the checkpoint holds 394 rows.) The near-100% quarantine is a SEPARATE calibration problem, not caused by the hang.
input.root_cause: CONFIRMED. The hang is the async OpenRouterClient being driven by a throwaway event loop per call inside worker threads. glm_transport._glm_generate wraps every judge call in asyncio.run(_run()) (glm_transport.py:52-72), and run_audit.py runs 9 of these concurrently in a ThreadPoolExecutor (run_audit.py:198-201). OpenRouterClient is a fully-async, SSE-streaming client: __init__ builds an httpx.AsyncClient (openrouter_client.py:1559,1567,1595), and every call streams via `async with self._client.stream(...)` + `async for line in response.aiter_lines()` (openrouter_client.py:1642,1772) plus asyncio.timeout()/wait_for child tasks. This design assumes ONE long-lived loop. asyncio.run() instead creates a fresh loop per call, then on exit cancels pending tasks and calls loop.shutdown_asyncgens() and closes the loop — but the SSE aiter_lines async-generators (and httpx's internal ones) are still live, so their aclose()/athrow cleanup coroutines are scheduled as `async_generator_athrow` tasks and then orphaned when the loop is destroyed -> the "Task was destroyed but it is pending!" flood (log full of `coro=<<async_generator_athrow without __name__>()>`). Because a brand-new AsyncClient + connection pool is also built and torn down every single call and cannot gracefully drain before the loop closes, sockets/fds and connection objects leak across all 9 threads. That leak is what actually degrades and finally wedges the run (the steady rate decay is the signature); the pending-task messages are the visible symptom of the same teardown-mid-stream defect. So yes: async client + asyncio.run() inside ThreadPoolExecutor workers is the root cause.
input.evidence: Log /tmp/.../scratchpad/audit_run.log: hundreds of "Task was destroyed but it is pending! task: <Task pending ... coro=<<async_generator_athrow without __name__>()>>"; progress lines show monotonic rate decay 1.80 -> 1.55 -> 1.10 -> 0.95/s (ETA 53m climbing to 94.5m) with errors=0 — a resource-leak slowdown, not a crash. glm_transport.py:72 `return asyncio.run(_run())`; :57 constructs a new OpenRouterClient inside every call. run_audit.py:198 ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=9) submitting audit_one -> harness.audit_card -> make_glm_runner. openrouter_client.py:1642 `async for line in response.aiter_lines()`, :1772 `async with self._client.stream(...)`, :1595 httpx.AsyncClient. Checkpoint tally (outputs/card_audit_checkpoint.jsonl, 394 rows): buckets {quarantined:393, kept_unchanged:1}; reason codes dominated by DETERMINISTIC Tier-0 gates (cot.unclassified_free_text=164, cache.source_counts_mismatch=116, corroborator.incomplete_binding=116, schema.duplicate_id=55) and report-AST faithfulness.span_does_not_entail_claim=194; only opus.transport_failed_after_retries=1. 91 cards were quarantined despite faith_verdict=PASS. => the mass quarantine is Tier-0/entailment calibration, essentially independent of the transport hang.
input.recommendation: (1) ROOT CAUSE: replace the per-call asyncio.run() of the async client with a synchronous transport in the worker; do NOT run the async SSE client on throwaway per-call loops under a ThreadPoolExecutor. (2) USABLE OUTPUT: essentially nothing usable as an AUDITED CARD SET — only 1 of 394 cards was kept, and 91 faith-PASS cards were quarantined, so the disposition/calibration output is not trustworthy; the 394 checkpoint rows ARE usable as diagnostic data (reason-code census). Treat the run as diagnostic-only. (3) RESUMABLE: yes — run_audit._load_done_rids() reads the checkpoint and pending is recomputed by re-deriving tier0.audit_row_id('', f'/{idx}', card) per index and skipping done rids (run_audit.py:81-93,186-191); the 394 rows persist, so a rerun resumes without re-auditing them. (Rows are appended under a lock and flushed per card, so the checkpoint is intact.) (4) CORRECT TRANSPORT: a SYNCHRONOUS per-call POST inside the worker — a plain requests/httpx.Client().post() to {OPENROUTER_BASE_URL}/chat/completions with the OpenRouter key, non-streaming (or sync-iter) — returning content + usage, shaped into the same claude-p-style envelope make_glm_runner already builds (result/model/modelUsage/is_error). No asyncio, no async generators, no per-call event loop, so nothing leaks and there is no loop-teardown-mid-stream. Fix SHAPE: rewrite glm_transport._glm_generate to do one blocking sync HTTP POST (reuse a thread-local or module httpx.Client for pooling); keep make_glm_runner's retry/fail-closed and envelope logic unchanged. If the async client must be reused, the only safe alternative is one persistent event loop per worker thread (created once, reused across calls, never closed per-call) — but a sync POST is simpler and matches a CPU-light judge call. Separately, before any real rerun, investigate the Tier-0/entailment over-quarantine (the glm-5.2 strict-entailment judge and the cot/cache/corroborator gates) since that, not the hang, is what makes the output unusable.
